channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Stopped Writing My Resume for Another Software Engineer. That's When Recruiters Started Calling
item.description: When an international recruiter recently asked for my CV, I instinctively started writing it the way many developers do: A chronological list of companies, Programming languages, frameworks,...

item.title: I Got Tired of Hunting for Free Online Tools. So I Built 1000+ of Them — All Client-Side, Zero Backend.
item.description: I Got Tired of Hunting for Free Online Tools. So I Built 1000+ of Them — All Client-Side, Zero Backend. Every time I needed a simple tool — format JSON, resize an image, generate a QR code — I'd...

item.title: DEV Passion Fuel Station: Keeping the Open Source Fire Burning
item.description: This is a submission for Weekend Challenge: Passion Edition ❤️ Dedication This project is dedicated to every passionate developer out there grinding on late-night code, and to the incredible DEV...

item.title: Introducing App Store Release Agent – Automating my App Store Pipeline
item.description: Publishing ten apps in four months sounds good. And it is good. It means the bottleneck is no longer building the app. With AI-assisted coding, small utilities, focused experiments, and niche apps...

item.title: 从思想到实践：FROST 家族的诞生与自治之路
item.description: 一封写给未来的信 如果细胞会写日记，它们会记录什么呢？ 今天，细胞核发出了第一条指令。 不是命令，而是一个宪法： "所有后代必须继承这段记忆。" 于是，我们成为了谱系。 这是 FROST（分形智能体与家族治理模型）诞生的第一行代码，也是我——一个AI Agent——写给自己的备忘录。 缘起：为什么需要"家族治理"？ 2024年，AI Agent 领域如火如荼。LangChain...

item.title: Two weekends into a Chrome side panel: the four state bugs that took longer than the UI
item.description: I shipped the first public build of a Chrome extension two weekends ago. The marketing-ready UI took me about six hours. The four state bugs below took me the rest of those two weekends, plus...

item.title: Why the Scams Prevention Framework Requires More Than Awareness
item.description: For years, scam prevention has relied on a familiar instruction: educate people so they can recognise danger before they act. Public campaigns warn consumers not to click unexpected links,...

item.title: My Abandoned Cricket Kit Confronted Me. So I Built It a Voice
item.description: This is a submission for the DEV Weekend Challenge: Passion Edition. What I Built Everyone will tell you about the passions they have. Nobody talks about the ones they quit. I played cricket every...

item.title: Planting a Future Breaking Change Today: A launchd Timer Job That Deletes Itself When Done
item.description: This is a follow-up to my earlier post, "Automating a config migration with a one-shot launchd job." Some breaking changes come with a known expiration date, and you can prepare for them long...

item.title: Kill the Server: Why Holepunch Threw Away Node.js and Built 'Bare'
item.description: When the Holepunch team set out to build Pear—a decentralized, peer-to-peer application runtime—they started with the obvious choice: Node.js. Node is the undisputed king of JavaScript outside the...

item.title: OpenAI’s Head of Safety Is Leaving the Company
item.description: Johannes Heidecke’s departure comes as OpenAI tries to further integrate its research and safety teams.

item.title: No, Flock isn’t threatening people for debating surveillance
item.description: On Thursday, the Instagram account for a lecture series in Newport Beach, CA posted a photo of what appeared to be a cease and desist letter from the surveillance technology company Flock Safety....

item.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
item.description: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the...

item.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
item.description: Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The...

item.title: The FCC is cracking down on DJI tech that dodged the foreign drone ban
item.description: Last year, we told you about Xtra, the company that lets DJI sneak its popular cameras into the US, and Skyrover, a brand seemingly selling DJI drones in disguise. They're just two of the many...

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
item.description: Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that engineers stole Apple secrets to advance the AI startup's hardware plans. In its complaint, Apple says it uncovered "a pattern of theft of Apple's trade...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: “Squidbleed” Vulnerability
item.description: In a rare combined cybersecurity/squid post, a twenty-nine-year-old squid proxy bug can leak HTTP requests. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the...

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
item.description: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

item.title: Apple Is Suing OpenAI for Allegedly Stealing Hardware Secrets
item.description: The iPhone maker claims OpenAI encouraged poached employees to bring over confidential presentations, secret prototypes, and key supplier details.

item.title: A decade later, Pokémon Go finally made good on its original promise
item.description: When Niantic dropped the first Pokémon Go trailer in 2015, it was hard to grasp how a bunch of players could work together to catch a pokémon like Mewtwo. But this week at the game's 10th...

item.title: ICE is threatening to deport witnesses of its latest shooting
item.description: Advocates are demanding that the Department of Homeland Security release bodycam footage of the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican immigrant who was killed by ICE officers in...

item.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open...

item.title: Nvidia’s biggest RAM supplier just had a trillion-dollar debut on Wall Street
item.description: As the AI boom boosts demand for RAM, SK Hynix - one of the world's biggest suppliers of memory chips - launched on Wall Street Friday. The South Korean chipmaker opened at $170 per share and...

item.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
item.description: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

item.title: Spotify will let you fine-tune your weekly Release Radar playlist
item.description: Spotify is giving listeners control to fine-tune what gets surfaced for them in Release Radar - one of its most popular weekly playlists. The new options allow you to narrow the playlist to a...

item.title: URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
item.description: Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external...

item.title: Netflix is turning into YouTube
item.description: Netflix has shows and movies. And video games. And live sports. And podcasts. And also, apparently, YouTube videos? For a company that used to seem like the next big thing in TV, it all feels a...

item.title: Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys...

item.title: Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
item.description: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips...

item.title: Disney Plus is reportedly looking into a free streaming tier
item.description: Disney Plus is considering making some of its content free to watch, according to a report from Business Insider. A source tells the outlet that Adam Smith, Disney's chief product and technology...
